mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals weakening labor demand and future earnings pressure. A month-over-month contraction in PAYEMS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress in response to deteriorating labor market fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131516.0 thousand as of October 2011 in post-crisis recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile labor market
failure_condition: Payroll contractions fail to precede negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) combined with stable or rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) creates a regime of negative real rates that incentivizes equity risk-taking. Persistence of this regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as portfolio reallocation into equities accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 226.955 as of November 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with inflation above 2%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) measure forward-looking construction demand and household formation expectations. A sustained decline in HOUST below 700 thousand units signals weakening demand momentum and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 658.0 thousand as of September 2011
failure_condition: HOUST < 700 fails to precede negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real economic capacity utilization. A month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as earnings expectations are immediately revised downward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines fail to precede negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly measure aggregate demand strength. When both RSAFS growth and GDPC1 growth are positive and accelerating, equity valuations expand due to earnings visibility. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 397666.0 million and GDPC1 at 13352.8 billion (2017 dollars) as of Q3 2011
regime_hint: expansion phase with positive demand momentum
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS × GDPC1) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when both series are growing.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations decline due to weakening consumer demand.
regime_hint: post-crisis labor market recovery with elevated structural unemployment
failure_condition: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp fail to precede negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at zero lower bound and CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, real rates become increasingly negative, creating a regime where equity risk premiums compress. This regime-specific mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as monetary accommodation is perceived as permanent.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 226.955 as of November 2011
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity and labor utilization. When both are declining simultaneously, the economy is in a supply-demand contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress from both reduced output and labor hoarding costs.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.1851 and PAYEMS at 131516.0 thousand as of September-October 2011
regime_hint: synchronized supply-labor contraction in post-crisis environment
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO × PAYEMS) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both series decline.
